subject: Sociology
title: School Funding Inequality
description: A 60-second lesson on School Funding Inequality.

hook.text: Some US schools have swimming pools. Others can't afford pencils. Both are called public education.

buildup.text: In many countries, school funding depends on local property taxes, creating vast disparities between districts.

discovery.text: Rich neighborhoods fund rich schools. Poor neighborhoods fund poor schools. Geography becomes destiny.

twist.text: Courts have ruled this unconstitutional multiple times. Yet the funding model persists in most US states.

climax.text: School funding inequality is perhaps the clearest example of how structure, not choice, creates educational gaps.

quiz.question: Why do school funding disparities exist?
options: Funding often depends on local property taxes
